text: AI Playbook and Toolkit for Public Health Departments

alt: AI Playbook for Public Health logo

text:
Welcome participants to the Agentic AI lesson.
Explain that this deck is designed for staff training, leadership orientation, governance preparation, or cross-program discussion.
Frame the topic in plain language: Agentic AI can coordinate multi-step workflows across systems under explicit rules and human oversight.
Invite participants to think about where this topic already appears in their department's workflows, vendor tools, data systems, communications, or planning conversations.
Agentic AI can coordinate multi-step workflows across systems under explicit rules and human oversight.
textPreview: Agentic AI can coordinate multi-step workflows across systems under explicit rules and human oversight.

text: Agentic AI can coordinate work across systems, but that makes it higher risk than simple drafting. In public health operations, even routing a task, prioritizing a signal, or preparing an outreach list can affect response time, workload,.

text: Agentic systems need strict boundaries, audit logs, and escalation paths.
textPreview: Agentic systems need strict boundaries, audit logs, and escalation paths.

Use this slide in the Agentic AI training module.
Use this slide to explain the practical public health reason for learning this topic before AI tools are approved or deployed.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 3, 11, 12, 13 and Tools 12, 14, 35, 17.
Emphasize the core guardrail: Agentic systems need strict boundaries, audit logs, and escalation paths.
Ask participants how this topic could affect their own program area, staff roles, community relationships, data, or public communications.
By the end of this lesson, staff should be able to:

• Explain Agentic AI in plain language.
• Identify appropriate public health uses and limits.
• Describe human review, governance, equity, privacy, and monitoring expectations.
• Connect the topic to the related plays and tools.

text:
Use this slide in the Agentic AI training module.
Walk through the objectives and connect each one to a practical implementation decision.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 3, 11, 12, 13 and Tools 12, 14, 35, 17.
Explain that participants do not need technical expertise to use this material. The goal is enough shared understanding to participate in governance, readiness assessment, use case review, procurement, deployment, or monitoring.

Agentic AI moves beyond drafting and summarizing by coordinating multiple steps toward a defined goal. In public health, this might mean monitoring incoming reports, gathering relevant data, preparing a draft brief, routing it to the right team, and updating a task queue. Because agentic systems can initiate actions, they require tighter boundaries, audit logs, role-based access, and human approval before consequential steps occur.

text: Definition: Agentic AI systems can analyze information, generate outputs, and take a sequence of actions toward a defined goal across multiple systems while following explicit rules and retaining human oversight.
textPreview: Definition: Agentic AI systems can analyze information, generate outputs, and take a sequence of actions toward a defined goal across mul...

Use this slide in the Agentic AI training module.
Use this slide to provide the narrative overview and definition before moving into detailed application.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 3, 11, 12, 13 and Tools 12, 14, 35, 17.
Reinforce this definition: Definition: Agentic AI systems can analyze information, generate outputs, and take a sequence of actions toward a defined goal across multiple systems while following explicit rules and retaining human oversight.

Internal workflows where the AI can gather, organize, and route information without contacting the public or making final decisions. Surveillance support tasks such as clustering incoming signals, preparing evidence packets, or flagging items.

text: Define allowable actions, prohibited actions, required human approvals, and emergency shutoff procedures. Maintain audit logs showing what the AI accessed, generated, changed, routed, or recommended. Limit access to the minimum systems and.
textPreview: Define allowable actions, prohibited actions, required human approvals, and emergency shutoff procedures. Maintain audit logs showing wha...

text: A named program or operational owner should remain accountable for the workflow. The AI should never obscure who made a public health decision or why a particular action was taken. Governance should approve agentic workflows before deployment.
textPreview: A named program or operational owner should remain accountable for the workflow. The AI should never obscure who made a public health dec...

text:
Use this slide in the Agentic AI training module.
Use this slide to turn the concept into practical behaviors, decisions, and safeguards.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 3, 11, 12, 13 and Tools 12, 14, 35, 17.
Invite participants to identify where these practices would need to be documented in their own department.
Start with Appropriate Starting Points, then compare it with the other practice areas on the slide.
Use the topic to examine real workflows, not abstract technology.
textPreview: Use the topic to examine real workflows, not abstract technology.

text: Classify incoming reports or alerts by urgency, topic, geography, and required response path, then route them to staff for confirmation.
textPreview: Classify incoming reports or alerts by urgency, topic, geography, and required response path, then route them to staff for confirmation.

text: Create task queues from incoming reports
textPreview: Create task queues from incoming reports

text: Transform email, forms, or case updates into structured follow-up tasks with owners, due dates, and escalation flags.
textPreview: Transform email, forms, or case updates into structured follow-up tasks with owners, due dates, and escalation flags.

text: Gather relevant reports, data extracts, literature, and prior decisions into a review packet before staff make a recommendation.
textPreview: Gather relevant reports, data extracts, literature, and prior decisions into a review packet before staff make a recommendation.

text:
Use this slide in the Agentic AI training module.
Use this slide to make the topic concrete through public health workflow examples.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 3, 11, 12, 13 and Tools 12, 14, 35, 17.
For each example, ask: What problem is being solved? What data would be used? Who reviews the AI output? What decision remains human? What could go wrong?

What actions may the AI take without approval, and what actions require human approval? What audit logs are needed to reconstruct what happened? How will staff pause, override, or escalate an agentic workflow?

text: Map a multi-step workflow such as outbreak signal review, inspection report preparation, or care coordination outreach. Mark which steps the AI may support, which steps require human approval, and which steps are prohibited.

Expected takeaways include: Agentic workflow boundary statement, Human approval and escalation map, Audit log and monitoring requirements.
